I'll help you find Linear issues that are marked as 'done' but aren't actually closed by any merged pull request. Let me start by exploring the table structures.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'linear_issues'"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'github_pull_requests'"}

text:
Now I need to find Linear issues that are 'done' and have at least one pull request that claims to close them (via closes_linear field), but aren't actually closed by any merged pull request. Let me break this down:

1. First, find all Line…(+279)

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    li.id as issue_id,
    li.title,
    COUNT(pr.number) as total_prs,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN pr.state = 'merged' THEN 1 END) as merged_prs
FROM linear_issues li
LEFT JOIN github_pull_requests pr ON li.id = pr.closes_linear
WHERE li.state = 'done'
GROUP BY li.id, li.title
HAVING COUNT(pr.number) > 0 AND COUNT(CASE WHEN pr.state = 'merged' THEN 1 END) = 0"}

data: {"rows":[{"issue_id":"ENG-167","title":"Billing webhook retries drop events","total_prs":1,"merged_prs":0},{"issue_id":"ENG-199","title":"Migrate auth to OAuth2 + PKCE","total_prs":1,"merged_prs":0}],"truncated":false,"touched":[{"name":"linear_issues","source":"virtual","access":"read","op":"select…(+205)

text: Perfect! I found 2 Linear issues that are marked as 'done' and have at least one pull request claiming to close them, but none of those pull requests are merged. Let me get the exact count and list all the issue IDs:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    COUNT(*) as count,
    STRING_AGG(li.id, ', ') as issue_ids
FROM linear_issues li
LEFT JOIN github_pull_requests pr ON li.id = pr.closes_linear
WHERE li.state = 'done'
GROUP BY li.id
HAVING COUNT(pr.number) > 0 AND COUNT(CASE WHEN pr.state = 'merged' THEN 1 END) = 0"}
